acceptance_criteria: Define a minimal 2-4 hour repeatable playable-slice contract using accepted M1-M4 systems without implementing production code.
Specify start state, player goal, success/failure conditions, required command surfaces, required read models, and replay evidence.

Explicitly preserve Human Gates for M5 formal passage, manual node battle decision, frozen product/platform/core architecture changes, telemetry, server/multiplayer, arbitrary-code mods, release/commercial choices, branding, and major cultural-risk decisions.
Do not add formal historical content, production dependencies, roadmap edits, acceptance-matrix edits, ADR edits, gate docs, release artifacts, or implementation files.
required_tests: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M5-SLICE-DEFINITION-001

target.name: M5 playable slice contract v0
target.intent: Define the smallest repeatable 2-4 hour playable slice that proves vassal networks, agent politics, population/labor pressure, monsoon campaign timing, logistics, succession pressure, and non-map-painting victory using accepted M1-M4 systems only.
target.historical_policy: No formal historical content is introduced by this contract. Any named scenario, real polity mapping, cultural symbol, language, person list, event, or asset direction must route to historical_researcher before production content.
target.implementation_policy: This task defines contract only. Product code, package code, content-source data, production dependencies, roadmap edits, acceptance-matrix edits, ADRs, gate docs, release artifacts, and implementation files remain out of scope.

start_state.world_basis: Accepted M1 deterministic scheduler/command/query/save envelope, M2 economy/population/route fixture substrate, M3 polity/office/character/vassal/postwar substrate, and M4 campaign/logistics/siege/withdrawal/AI substrate.

polities.note: Labels are gameplay placeholders, not final historical identities.

map_shape.starting_hypothesis: 12-18 political centers and 30-48 districts, with one river trunk, one land bottleneck, one fortified target, and two alternate supply origins.
map_shape.validation_metric: The player can read control, suzerain relation, route risk, and target state without opening developer tools.
map_shape.validation_way: M5 client UI Playwright no-overlap checks plus M5 player research comprehension notes.

calendar.starting_hypothesis: Start in a dry campaign window with a monsoon transition inside the session.
calendar.validation_metric: At least one plan becomes better, delayed, or cancelled because route/supply/season reasons change.
calendar.validation_way: M5 sim replay, AI trace, and player research reason-code review.

resources.starting_hypothesis: Player has enough cash, grain, labor, and obligations for one credible campaign, not enough to ignore harvest and transport constraints.
resources.validation_metric: Players face at least one real tradeoff between mobilizing households, reserving grain, relying on a vassal, or delaying campaign start.
resources.validation_way: M5 sim acceptance tests and player research decision logs.

political_pressure.starting_hypothesis: One office appointment, one vassal obligation, one succession or ruler-availability pressure, and one postwar governance choice are active within the slice.
political_pressure.validation_metric: At least three non-identical viable governance/campaign routes can complete or partially complete the goal.
political_pressure.validation_way: M5 player research, deterministic branch replays, and postwar comparison read models.

player_goal.primary_goal: By the end of the session, secure and preserve recognition over the contested target through a campaign or negotiated postwar arrangement, then keep the arrangement stable long enough to show obligation, administration, and loyalty consequences.
player_goal.non_painting_rule: A district changing actual controller is insufficient. Success requires an auditable postwar or vassal arrangement, source-ledger costs, and no unresolved breach that makes the result immediately void.

session_length.starting_hypothesis: 2-4 hours for a first playable pass, 60-120 minutes for a repeat pass after familiarity.
session_length.validation_metric: Median time to reach success/failure, number of forced restarts, and whether players can explain why the result happened.
session_length.validation_way: M5-PLAYER-RESEARCH-001 manual/anonymized observations; no telemetry or network collection.

player_decisions.decision: Choose and appoint a campaign or district agent before committing to war.
player_decisions.advantage: A better-fit agent improves execution reliability, forecast quality, or local compliance for a specific office or policy.
player_decisions.cost: The appointment consumes political capacity, can displace another office holder, can raise administrative burden, and may empower an agent with local interests.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires eligible office, visible candidate reasons, jurisdiction/policy context, and protocol command preview.
player_decisions.counter: A rival or local bloc can exploit strained relationships, succession support, overburdened administration, or a misfit policy to reduce compliance.
player_decisions.design_guard: Characters are not total attribute cards. Public ability values are context inputs; interests, relationships, office fit, information, and reason codes must drive the decision.

player_decisions.decision: Ask a minor polity or vassal for troops, grain, access, storage, or a lighter obligation package.
player_decisions.advantage: Obligations convert political recognition into concrete campaign support without requiring direct annexation.
player_decisions.cost: Heavy obligations increase arrears, breach risk, relationship damage, and future refusal pressure through explicit obligation accounting.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires valid suzerain relation, no suzerain cycle, obligation source ledger, due/cadence rules, and visible fulfillment history.
player_decisions.counter: The vassal can partially fulfill, defer, refuse, or breach; a rival can target the same minor through visible weakness or delayed protection.
player_decisions.design_guard: No culture or ethnicity has a fixed obedience buff. Compliance must come from obligation terms, history, distance, protection, interests, and current constraints.

player_decisions.decision: Commit households, retainers, grain, and cash to build campaign readiness.
player_decisions.advantage: Early mobilization and grain reservation increase safe operating days and reduce campaign collapse risk.
player_decisions.cost: Mobilized households reduce seasonal labor, delayed release can waste grain, and professional retainers consume cash and grain over time.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires M2 population/resource state, M4 grain source ledger, explicit reservation/consumption/release, and harvest/season read models.
player_decisions.counter: The rival can wait out supplies, season risk can spoil route/grain assumptions, and over-mobilization can weaken the next harvest.
player_decisions.design_guard: No hidden global penalty. Every consequence must attach to source ledgers, local labor, harvest impact, grain loss, or visible reason codes.

player_decisions.decision: Pick campaign objective, route, transport origin, and start window.
player_decisions.advantage: A strong route/window can reach the target before monsoon risk, enemy reinforcement, or supply exhaustion.
player_decisions.cost: Planning ties up commitments, exposes intent, can become stale, and may need cancellation before sunk costs become worse.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires faction knowledge age, route/season forecast, muster readiness, grain forecast, and start-window reason codes.
player_decisions.counter: Enemy knowledge, delayed muster, river/road capacity shifts, and changing season risk can make waiting or cancellation correct.
player_decisions.design_guard: Monsoon is not a global debuff. It must alter specific route capacity, disease/siege/supply risk, river conditions, or timing reasons.

player_decisions.decision: Commit to march, automatic engagement, siege choice, withdrawal, or negotiated end.
player_decisions.advantage: A well-timed campaign can create a postwar candidate and shift recognition without direct permanent annexation.
player_decisions.cost: Casualties, supply loss, credit hooks, siege delay, breach damage, and withdrawal consequences flow back into economy, loyalty, and obligations.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires active campaign plan, march state, supply state, siege or field engagement state, and deterministic combat/siege resolution.
player_decisions.counter: The defender can benefit from time, fortification, supply exhaustion, relief risk, or the attacker's need to withdraw before monsoon.
player_decisions.design_guard: Manual node battle remains out of scope. Automatic engagement and siege choices must remain deterministic and replayable.

player_decisions.decision: Choose direct control, restored vassal ruler, or tribute-only settlement after a campaign result.
player_decisions.advantage: Different arrangements convert victory into different mixes of control, obligation, continuity, and risk.
player_decisions.cost: Direct control needs garrison/admin capacity, restored vassal keeps local autonomy and future breach risk, tribute-only has lower control and weaker military leverage.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires valid postwar candidate, no duplicate same-district settlement, M3 obligation compatibility, and 24-month comparison preview.
player_decisions.counter: Local elites, arrears, administrative overload, succession pressure, or a rival's protection offer can undermine the arrangement.
player_decisions.design_guard: Victory is not color fill. The result must remain a political arrangement with auditable terms and future failure modes.

player_decisions.decision: Respond to ruler unavailability or succession pressure before it breaks appointments, obligations, or vassal recognition.
player_decisions.advantage: A timely settlement can preserve a vassal network or keep a campaign chain legitimate after a leadership shock.
player_decisions.cost: Supporting one claimant can damage another relationship, expose hostage/relationship dependencies, or change office execution.
player_decisions.dependencies: Requires active succession/crisis read model, candidate support sources, relationship data, and reason-code preview.
player_decisions.counter: Rivals and local powers can contest legitimacy, exploit weak hostages/relationships, or refuse to inherit old obligations.
player_decisions.design_guard: Succession is a network reevaluation, not a global stability number.

authoritative_state: M1 WorldState, GameDay, deterministic RNG, GameCommand/Event/Query v1, save envelope, replay tail, and state hash.
M2 district, economy, population, labor, grain/cash, route, agriculture, and seasonal state.
M3 polity, suzerain chain, obligations, offices, characters, appointment policies, administrative burden, succession, and postwar governance state.
M4 campaign objective, faction knowledge, muster, grain supply, transport forecast, march/supply, engagement, siege, withdrawal, war outcome, postwar candidate, and AI trace state.
state.client_state_rule: Client and UI may hold read models and UI selection state only; they must not own or mutate authoritative WorldState.
state.save_rule: Slice save/load must use accepted save envelope and explicit protocol/load rejection behavior; no irreversible migration is authorized.

daily: GameDay advance, march movement, supply consumption, siege pressure, route/season risk, AI campaign decision opportunity, state hash update.
monthly_or_periodic: Administrative burden projection, obligation due/arrears review, labor/population recovery, harvest or resource cycle, office/policy execution summaries.
event_driven: Appointment, obligation creation/fulfillment, campaign objective create/update/cancel, muster response, grain reserve/release, start march, field engagement, siege choice, withdrawal, postwar arrangement, succession resolution, save/load.

formula_intent: Expose formula consequences through reason codes, deltas, and source-ledger rows rather than hidden global modifiers.

Make each advantage local and conditional: offices improve specific execution paths; obligations provide specific resources; routes change timing and risk; postwar methods shift control/obligation/admin tradeoffs.

Costs should be delayed when appropriate but still attributable to concrete ledgers such as labor, grain, cash, obligation arrears, administrative burden, credit/reliability hooks, casualties, or relationship/support reason codes.
Do not use culture, ethnicity, or modern identity categories as fixed mechanical buffs or penalties.

Do not reduce characters to one aggregate score. Public ability values support office fit, but relationships, interests, information, health/status, local power base, and institutional policy must remain visible factors.
Difficulty and AI behavior must not rely on hidden resource cheats. Any future difficulty advantage requires explicit listing and must preserve core rules.
invariants: Same seed, same start state, and same command sequence produce the same final state hash in Node runner and Worker/browser host.
Every player and AI state change uses versioned GameCommandV1 or explicit system scheduler command paths.
No Math.random, Date.now, object iteration ordering dependence, or unspecified tie-breaker may affect simulation results.
No UI, renderer, Electron main, or client store mutates authoritative WorldState directly.
One district has one actual controller at a time; suzerain chains are acyclic; obligations are auditable and cannot be double-counted.
M2 resource changes conserve explicit source ledgers; troop/tribute fulfillment cannot create grain, cash, labor, or headcount from nothing.
Postwar arrangements cannot duplicate or replace same-district settlement paths without explicit accepted rules.
Campaign cancellation, withdrawal, siege, and postwar results leave reason-coded evidence and cannot erase already-paid costs.
AI trace size remains bounded and serializable.
Manual node battle, telemetry, server/multiplayer, and arbitrary-code mods remain absent.

ai_behavior.parity: AI submits the same GameCommandV1 command kinds as the player and can also decide no-action/wait when no command is justified.
minimum_behaviors: Create or update one visible campaign objective when knowledge supports it.
Wait with reason codes for supply shortfall, seasonal risk, stale knowledge, or missing muster.
Start a march only when muster, route, and grain forecasts are ready.
Reinforce or partially answer obligations using muster response paths.
Continue, cancel, withdraw, or change objective with explicit trace reasons.
Avoid a postwar action when high postwar risk is visible instead of blindly expanding.
constraints: No full diplomacy AI, grand strategy personality simulation, hidden map knowledge, background telemetry, server coordination, or arbitrary-code scripting.
No culture/identity fixed preferences. AI preferences must derive from visible state, obligations, relationships, knowledge age, logistics, and risk.
evidence: M5-MULTIYEAR-AI-RUN-001 must record bounded trace summaries for wait/no-action/withdraw/target-change decisions.
M5 player-facing UI must expose primary AI reason codes where they affect player understanding.

required_explanations: Current goal and remaining success/failure conditions.
Top three reason codes for appointment, obligation, campaign forecast, route/supply risk, AI action, siege state, withdrawal, and postwar arrangement.
Before/after preview for commands that change offices, obligations, campaign plans, grain reservations, siege choice, withdrawal, or postwar governance.
Replay/save evidence: current day, seed or scenario id, command count, replay checkpoint, and current state hash.
Out-of-scope indicators where needed: manual node battle is unavailable in M5 unless Human Gate later authorizes it.
ui_explanation.read_model_rule: UI explanations must be projected from protocol query/read models and reason codes; client code must not duplicate simulation formulas.
ui_explanation.accessibility_and_layout: M5-CLIENT-PLAYABLE-UI-001 must verify text does not overlap at supported desktop viewports and that dense decision panels remain scannable.

success_conditions: Player reaches a postwar or vassal arrangement for the contested target using accepted M3/M4 command paths.

The arrangement remains valid through the required stabilization window with no unresolved duplicate settlement, suzerain cycle, unpaid mandatory source-ledger violation, or immediate authority collapse.

The session records at least one appointment decision, one labor/grain/logistics decision, one route/window decision, one campaign or cancellation decision, and one postwar or vassal obligation decision.
Final replay evidence includes start hash, command sequence identity, final hash, save/load round-trip, and matching Node/Worker results.
Player research can show that at least a majority of observed players can state why they succeeded, failed, waited, withdrew, or cancelled without needing formulas.
failure_conditions: Player court loses every maintainable power center or no eligible successor/political continuity path remains.
The campaign collapses from supply, season, siege, or withdrawal consequences and no valid political arrangement is made by the slice end.
The target changes actual control but lacks any valid postwar/vassal arrangement, obligation term, or stabilization evidence.
Mandatory obligations or administrative burden enter an unrecoverable breach state caused by player decisions before success is locked.
Replay/save/load hashes diverge, an invariant fails, or an authoritative state mutation bypasses GameCommand paths.
A required Human Gate decision is encountered and not explicitly approved; the task must stop rather than treat it as success.
replay_evidence: Named scenario id, content/fixture manifest hash, seed, start day, start state hash, and platform/host.
Ordered command ids with actor kind, command kind, preview/submit result, event tail, and rejection reason where relevant.
Save export/import or load-save checkpoint at minimum before campaign start and after postwar arrangement.
Node runner and Worker/browser final state hash equality for the same command sequence.
AI trace summary containing selected candidate, primary reason, bounded candidate list, and no-action/wait/withdraw/target-change counts.
Player research evidence records manual/anonymized observations only; no telemetry or network collection.

parameters.name: session_length_minutes
parameters.metric: Observed time to success/failure and number of abandoned sessions.

parameters.name: repeat_session_minutes
parameters.metric: Observed repeat pass time and whether players choose a different viable route.

parameters.name: in_game_duration_days
parameters.starting_hypothesis: 540-900 days, enough for one campaign window, one monsoon transition, one harvest pressure, and postwar stabilization.
parameters.metric: At least one season-driven plan change and one postwar outcome comparison occur.

parameters.name: decision_count
parameters.starting_hypothesis: 6-10 meaningful player decisions on a successful path.
parameters.metric: Decisions with distinct cost/dependency/counter evidence, not clicks that only confirm a linear script.
parameters.validation_way: Command log review and player research.

parameters.name: polity_and_map_scale
parameters.starting_hypothesis: 2 major polities, 3 minor polities, 30-48 districts, 12-18 centers.
parameters.metric: UI remains readable and Node/Worker replay stays within M5 performance budget.

parameters.name: ai_multiyear_duration
parameters.starting_hypothesis: At least 3 in-game years without rescue commands.
parameters.metric: No invariant failures, bounded rejection rate, bounded trace size, final hash repeatability.

parameters.name: success_comprehension_rate
parameters.starting_hypothesis: Most observed players can describe the top reasons for their outcome after the session.
parameters.metric: Manual research notes: understood goal, next action, risk, and outcome reasons.

boundaries: No formal M5 passage. M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 remains the formal Human Gate.
No manual node battle, tactical battle UI, 3D battle, or complex naval warfare.
No frozen product, platform, or core architecture change.
No telemetry, analytics pipeline, accounts, paid services, secrets, server, multiplayer, remote collection, or arbitrary-code mod support.
No release, commercial, store, branding, logo, trademark, signing, installer, public distribution, MANIFEST, SHA256SUMS, or VALIDATION_REPORT work.
No formal historical names, claims, identities, cultural symbols, art, music, or language content without historical_researcher routing.

exploits.counter: Preview is allowed, but submitted plans, commitments, and reservations must leave reason-coded opportunity or source-ledger consequences when accepted.

exploits.risk: Infinite vassal support through repeated obligation fulfillment.
exploits.counter: M3 obligation accounting and M4 muster commitments must reject double counting and preserve source ledgers.

exploits.risk: Direct control becomes a dominant reward.
exploits.counter: Direct control must carry visible admin/garrison/opportunity costs and be compared against restored-vassal and tribute-only outcomes.

exploits.risk: Monsoon becomes a generic punishment instead of planning.
exploits.counter: Route, river, supply, siege, disease, or timing reasons must explain the effect and allow planning around it.

exploits.risk: Assault or siege choice gives free progress.
exploits.counter: Siege progress must expose casualties, supply, morale, credit, surrender, or withdrawal reasons.
Office fit must include relationship, interest, local base, policy continuity, status, and eligibility reasons, not only public ability values.

exploits.risk: AI uses hidden knowledge or free resources.
exploits.counter: AI trace must cite visible FactionKnowledge, obligation, supply, route, and campaign read-model inputs and submit normal GameCommands.

exploits.risk: Player treats map color as victory.
exploits.counter: Success requires valid arrangement, obligation/admin evidence, and stabilization; actual control alone is not enough.

vertical_slice_acceptance: M5-SLICE-DEFINITION-001 reaches REVIEW with this contract and systems_architect review requested; gameplay_designer does not self-approve implementation.
M5-SIM-PLAYABLE-LOOP-001 implements the contract through accepted authoritative sim/protocol/save paths and proves deterministic replay.
M5-CLIENT-PLAYABLE-UI-001 lets the player inspect goal, issue commands, preview consequences, save/load, and understand reason codes without holding WorldState in client state.
M5-WEB-WINDOWS-DISTRIBUTABLE-001 proves local Web and unsigned Windows smoke distributability without release/commercial/branding/security drift.
M5-MULTIYEAR-AI-RUN-001 proves multiple AI years without rescue, hidden telemetry, or command parity breach.
M5-PERFORMANCE-GATE-001 reports concrete Web, Windows, Node, save/load, and read-model measurements.
M5-PLAYER-RESEARCH-001 records manual/anonymized observations about comprehension, 2-4 hour repeatability, and product-pillar proof.
M5-MANUAL-NODE-BATTLE-DECISION-001 and M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 remain explicit Human Gates; no reviewer or CI result can auto-pass them.

gameplay_designer.responsibility: primary gameplay_designer writer for bounded M5 playable-slice contract

systems_architect.responsibility: systems_architect review of bounded M5 playable-slice contract

qa_reviewer.responsibility: independent read-only QA review of bounded M5 playable-slice contract

notes: This narrows M5 scope before implementation evidence tasks.
If the contract requires a frozen architecture, platform, branding, commercial, release, telemetry, networking, mod, or manual battle decision, stop and route to Human Gate.
